expectedExtraction.clinicalNotesSummary: No PT and no NSAID despite prolonged knee symptoms.

actualExtraction.clinicalNotesSummary: No physical therapy and no nsaid despite prolonged knee symptoms.

extractionFieldBreakdown.expected: No PT and no NSAID despite prolonged knee symptoms.
extractionFieldBreakdown.actual: No physical therapy and no nsaid despite prolonged knee symptoms.

perCase.decisionReasoning: The patient is a 63-year-old with a diagnosis of primary osteoarthritis of the right knee (M17.12) requesting a total knee arthroplasty (CPT 27447) with 30 weeks of documented symptoms. While age eligibility (AGE_MINIMUM_002) is satisfied, the request fails the conservative care requirement (CONSERVATIVE_CARE_001). Clinical notes confirm that neither physical therapy nor NSAIDs have been trialed, despite the prolonged symptom duration. Kaiser Permanente WA's TKA Clinical Review Criteria explicitly require documented failure of conservative therapies — including physical therapy and NSAIDs — as prerequisites for medical necessity. Although Premera Blue Cross removed its mandatory formal PT requirement (6 visits over 12 weeks) as of July 1, 2016, the absence of any NSAID trial remains a gap under both payers' frameworks. The failure to attempt or document failure of these foundational conservative treatments represents a material unmet criterion. A likely_deny outcome is warranted unless the provider can supply clinical justification for why PT and/or NSAIDs were contraindicated or otherwise inappropriate for this patient.
